discovery.run_summary: Mixed tape with mega-cap AI names (GOOGL, AMZN, ORCL, AVGO, MSFT) hit hard on AI competition/researcher-departure narratives while small caps (IWM +0.88%) and biotech (XBI +3.65%) outperform. Two high-quality discoveries today: AVAV with a textbook bad-news bundle 8-K (restatement + officer departure) one week before earnings, and ALGN with an officer-departure 8-K driving an outsized move. Defense names KTOS/AVAV selling off together suggests sympathy compounding the AVAV-specific damage.

notable_index_moves: XLC -2.37% and XLY -1.89% as GOOGL (-5%) and AMZN (-4.75%) drag on AI-researcher-exit narrative
XBI +3.65% — strong biotech bid (BEAM, BBIO participating)
SMH +1.37% diverging from megacap tech weakness; ORCL/AVGO -5%/-4.7% on AI capex worries
Small caps (IWM +0.88%) outperforming, VIX still benign at 17

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Restatement 8-K (Item 4.02) bundled with officer departure (5.02) one week before earnings
discoveries.thesis: Item 4.02 — non-reliance on previously issued financials — is the single most severe 8-K item code, and pairing it with a 5.02 officer departure days before a scheduled earnings print is textbook bad-news bundling. A ~11% one-day drop on a defense name with this filing combo is, if anything, restrained — investors who think they're buying the dip may be underestimating that prior financials are no longer trustworthy and earnings in 7 days are now an open question (possible delay, possible going-concern language, possible auditor change).
discoveries.what_confirms: Earnings on 6/29 delayed or pre-announced; auditor change disclosed; magnitude of restatement quantified as material; CFO (not just a director) named in 5.02
discoveries.what_kills: Restatement turns out to be narrow/technical (e.g., reclassification with no income statement impact) and the 5.02 departure is a planned retirement with successor named — in which case the drop is the overreaction and snaps back
discoveries.what_to_learn: Item 4.02 filings almost never end after one day of selling; the restatement process itself takes weeks and typically produces a second leg down when the magnitude is quantified. Fading the first-day drop is historically a losing trade.
discoveries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-22 with Item 4.02 (restatement) and 5.02 (officer change); earnings scheduled 6/29 after-hours
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 4.02 + 5.02 combination on same filing, 7 days before earnings — the prompt itself flags 4.02 as 'almost always severely bearish'
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibit to identify which periods are being restated and the estimated magnitude
Confirm whether the 6/29 earnings date is still scheduled or has been pulled
Identify which officer departed — CFO is far worse than a director
Check whether auditor (and audit committee chair) is unchanged
Watch KTOS and other defense-name sympathy moves for read-across vs. AVAV-specific damage

discoveries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Officer-departure 8-K (5.02) plus Reg FD disclosure on a beaten-down name in a green healthcare tape
discoveries.thesis: ALGN dropping ~4.5% on a 5.02 + 7.01 combo while XLV is up 0.44% and XBI up 3.65% suggests the market is reading the departure as material. But 5.02 without an accompanying 2.02 (earnings) or 4.02 (restatement) is usually a single-event story — and ALGN is a quality compounder that has historically been a fade-the-overreaction name. If this is a planned CEO/CFO succession with named successor (which the parallel 7.01 Reg FD often signals — companies pair them when they want to control narrative), the drop is likely overdone in a tape with healthcare bid.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibit shows planned retirement with named internal successor and clear transition timeline; no change to guidance in the Reg FD disclosure
discoveries.what_kills: Departure is the CFO mid-quarter with no successor named, OR the Reg FD disclosure contains a guidance cut — either makes the drop justified or even insufficient
discoveries.what_to_learn: 5.02 paired with 7.01 (Reg FD) on the same day frequently indicates a managed succession announcement rather than a surprise — companies use the Reg FD to set the narrative. Worth checking whether the pairing pattern has predictive value.
discoveries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-22 with Items 5.02 (officer change) and 7.01 (Reg FD)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Same-day 5.02 + 7.01 pairing on a quality compounder selling off in a green sector tape
research_pointers: Identify who departed — CEO/CFO vs. a divisional officer changes the read entirely
Read the Reg FD exhibit for any guidance commentary
Compare to prior ALGN officer-departure events and their reversal patterns
Check options skew for whether market is pricing follow-through or single-day shock

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
discoveries.setup: Defense-peer sympathy selloff with no company-specific 8-K and no scheduled earnings
discoveries.thesis: KTOS down ~5.8% with empty catalyst_signals on a day AVAV (the most comparable small-drone-defense peer) cratered on company-specific restatement news. ITA down only 1.46% — KTOS is selling at ~4x the sector move on what appears to be AVAV contagion. The accounting issue at AVAV is not a sector-wide read-across; investors conflating them creates an asymmetric fade setup once AVAV's filing is properly digested.
discoveries.what_confirms: No KTOS-specific bad news emerges over next 1-2 sessions; AVAV-specific framing settles; KTOS recovers as ITA stabilizes
discoveries.what_kills: KTOS files its own negative 8-K; defense budget headlines turn negative; AVAV restatement reveals an industry-wide accounting practice issue (unlikely but possible)
discoveries.what_to_learn: Single-name accounting blow-ups in a narrow industry often drag the nearest comp 1:1 for a day before the idiosyncratic nature becomes clear — these sympathy moves are among the more reliable fades.
discoveries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; appears to be sympathy with AVAV restatement news
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + magnitude far exceeds ITA sector move + closest industry peer AVAV down ~11% on restatement
research_pointers: Verify no KTOS-specific 8-K or news after market close
Compare KTOS revenue recognition practices to AVAV's restated areas once disclosed
Track KTOS vs. ITA over next 2-3 sessions for mean reversion
Check short interest and options activity for signs of forced selling

discoveries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
discoveries.setup: Coking coal name continuing a multi-day slide with only a Reg FD 8-K from four days prior
discoveries.thesis: The 6/18 Reg FD filing predates today's move and the 7.01 code alone tells us nothing about content. Coking coal is commodity-price driven and AMR has been weak for five days. Without knowing what the Reg FD disclosed or where met coal prices are trading, neither bull nor bear case clears the conf-3 bar.
discoveries.catalyst: Possibly the 6/18 Reg FD disclosure; possibly met coal commodity pricing
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 7.01 Reg FD content unknown; weakness predates today by multiple sessions
research_pointers: Read the 6/18 Reg FD exhibit for the actual disclosure content
Check met coal benchmark prices and Chinese steel demand commentary
Compare to peers HCC, METC to confirm whether this is name-specific or commodity-driven

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics
discoveries.setup: Biotech continuation move on strong XBI day with no company-specific 8-K
discoveries.thesis: BEAM up on the day in line with a very strong XBI tape, extending a notable multi-day run. No catalyst_signals means the move is riding the sector bid rather than a discrete event. Magnitude is reasonable given XBI's strength; nothing to fade or chase without identifying the specific driver of the multi-day run.
discoveries.what_confirms: XBI sustains bid; sector rotation into biotech continues
discoveries.what_kills: XBI rolls over; BEAM gives back gains faster than the sector
discoveries.catalyst: Sector rotation into biotech; possible takeout/data speculation not in 8-K stream
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move aligns with broad XBI strength
research_pointers: Check for biotech-conference-circuit chatter (ASCO follow-on, EHA data)
Review BEAM-specific pipeline catalysts in next 4-8 weeks
Monitor whether the multi-day move attracts a short report or takeout rumor

research_pointers: Check Onto exposure to HBM advanced packaging vs lithography peers
Cross-reference with AEIS move (also AI power/semicap adjacent, +4.2%) — suggests a sector AI-capex bid today
Watch for Nvidia GB300 shipment cadence commentary

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Advanced Energy supplies power conversion for semicap and data center; GB300 ramp + neocloud capex commitments support both end markets.
research_pointers: AEIS data center power product exposure
Note volume is below average — move may be momentum not conviction

ai_announcements.headline: Google DeepMind + A24 $75M AI filmmaking deal
ai_announcements.summary: Generative AI tools for film production. Modest dollars but signals studio adoption.

ai_announcements.headline: Groq raises $650M post Nvidia $20B not-acqui-hire, leans into neocloud
ai_announcements.summary: Groq survives as independent neocloud despite Nvidia talent deal; reinforces that Nvidia ecosystem is absorbing alternatives, not displacing them.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Largest movers today (AVAV -10.8%, FIZZ -6.7%, AMR -5.8%, KTOS -5.8%, ALGN -4.6%) are NOT AI-driven. AVAV has an 8-K item 4.02 (restatement) + 5.02 (officer departure) — severe accounting event, not AI. ALGN has 5.02 officer departure. AMR is coal (commodity). FIZZ is beverages (consumer). KTOS defense sympathy with AVAV likely. None of these belong in an AI-irrational-selloff bucket. Real AI signal today is the Nvidia-ecosystem capex bid lifting ONTO/AEIS, which appears rational rather than overdone.
